title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 28 January 2025.

items.title: **Xbox** boss Spencer says Series S compatibility requirement isn’t going away
bullets: No plans to allow the release of Series X exclusive games

items.title: **Xbox** has enlisted a veteran Gears of War studio to co-develop the next series entry
bullets: Gears of War: Judgement studio People Can Fly is co-developing Gears of War: E-Day

items.title: **Ubisoft** announces studio closure as it lays off 185 staff
bullets: Founded in 2002 as FreeStyleGames by a group of former Rare and Codemasters veterans, Ubisoft Leamington had most recently worked as a support studio on the Tom Clancy's The Division series, and also assisted development on games such as Star Wars Outlaws, Skull and Bones and Far Cry 5.

A small number of Leamington staff will be retained under remote contracts. Additionally, Ubisoft offices in Düsseldorf (formerly Blue Byte), Stockholm and the Newcastle-based Ubisoft Reflections will be downsized.

items.title: Dauntless and Fae Farm studio **Phoenix Labs** lays off "majority" of employees
bullets: The developer has faced multiple rounds of layoffs since its acquisition by blockchain company Forte Labs in 2023, starting with job cuts impacting 9 percent of its workforce in May that year. A further 34 layoffs followed seven months later, with an additional 140 jobs being cut last May. Layoffs have continued into 2025.
The studio says it'll share more about the fate of Dauntless and Fae Farm "in the coming weeks"
